published_prior_art.cite: LuShiFangZhangDing2025BipartiteDesign, arXiv:2501.09844, gives the common-p bipartite Hajek variance/CLT/conservative-variance benchmark; this is a sanity reduction, not a novelty claim.

repo_prior_art.cite: No catalogue entry gives a bipartite heterogeneous-Bernoulli Hajek graph-only variance envelope; exp_design_pm1_exactness_boundary_v1 is a one-mode covariance-implementability problem.

published_prior_art.cite: LuShiFangZhangDing2025BipartiteDesign treats iid Bernoulli(p) and explicitly leaves independent Bernoulli(p_k) analysis to future work; HarshawSavjeEisenstatMirrokniPougetAbadie2021BipartiteERL, arXiv:2103.06392, is linear exposure-response model based.

repo_prior_art.cite: exp_rollout_chebyshev_minimax optimizes rollout measurement fractions and exp_design_pm1_exactness_boundary_v1 optimizes covariance implementability; neither chooses heterogeneous bipartite intervention-unit Bernoulli probabilities for a Hajek envelope.

published_prior_art.cite: Viviano2026NetworkDesign, arXiv:2003.08421, optimizes pilot-estimated one-mode network precision; no checked paper solves this graph-only bipartite heterogeneous-p conservative-envelope program.

repo_prior_art.cite: exp_ego_cluster_exposure_clt contains an exposure-support-to-dependency-graph CLT package, but not the optimized heterogeneous bipartite Hajek design object.

published_prior_art.cite: LuShiFangZhangDing2025BipartiteDesign proves the homogeneous bipartite Hajek CLT; Leung2019ApproxNeighborhoodInterference, arXiv:1911.07085, proves IPW asymptotic normality under non-identical Bernoulli assignment in a different one-mode ANI setting.

repo_prior_art.cite: No catalogue entry gives graph-only post-design conservative Wald inference for optimized heterogeneous bipartite Hajek assignment.

published_prior_art.cite: LuShiFangZhangDing2025BipartiteDesign gives homogeneous conservative variance; HarshawMiddletonSavje2021OptimizedVariance, arXiv:2112.01709, and ParkWager2026NeymanJackknife, arXiv:2604.24017, give adjacent conservative-variance frameworks.

repo_prior_art.cite: The closest accepted design entries leave different open objects: Chebyshev exact nested-rollout minimax and pm1 covariance phase frontiers, not a reciprocal-product Bernoulli surrogate certificate.

published_prior_art.cite: UganderKarrerBackstromKleinberg2013GraphClusterRandomization, arXiv:1305.6979, and UganderYin2020RandomizedGCR, arXiv:2009.02297, give graph-cluster variance controls, not a constant-factor certificate for heterogeneous bipartite Bernoulli envelope design.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: headline / thm:convex-design
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The optimality claim is envelope-optimal for the graph-only conservative objective V_env, not exact minimax optimality for the true design variance over bounded potential outcomes.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The design contribution is a conservative-envelope optimal heterogeneous Bernoulli design under the stated budget and positivity constraints.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:hetero-clt / thm:postdesign-wald
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The inference results are conditional on bounded overlap-dependency and nondegenerate asymptotic variance at the selected p_n^*(G_n).
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Coverage is pointwise along graph/potential-outcome sequences satisfying those primitive regularity and nondegeneracy conditions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a small worked bipartite graph where the KKT system yields a heterogeneous p_n^*(G_n) that strictly improves V_env over the common-p design.
State a boundary corollary showing exact reduction to Lu et al. when the budget forces p_k = B_n/m_n for all k, including the Wald interval specialization.
Investigate whether V_env is sharp or constant-factor sharp as a worst-case bounded-outcome variance envelope; a positive result would move the design object closer to minimax.
Try weakening bounded overlap-dependency to a growing local-dependence condition closer to Aronow-Samii/Leung while preserving the denominator and CLT rates.

recommended_next_step: Advance this field-tier angle to Stage 0 with the envelope-optimal and nondegenerate-sequence caveats bound as scope.
